ddls:
CREATE TABLE "audio_clips" (
  "clip_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audio clip */,
  "path" TEXT /* File path to the audio clip */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the source of the data */,
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the actor in the audio clip */,
  "gender_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the gender of the actor */,
  "intensity_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the intensity of the emotion */,
  "statement_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the statement number */,
  "repetition_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the repetition number */,
  "emotion_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the emotion type */,
  "label_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the overall label */,
  "duration" REAL /* Duration of the audio clip in seconds */,
  "recording_date" TEXT /* Date when the audio clip was recorded */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number of the dataset */,
  "quality_rating" INTEGER /* Quality rating of the audio clip (1-5) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments about the audio clip */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("clip_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audio_clips_label_id FOREIGN KEY ("label_id") REFERENCES labels ("label_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audio_clips_statement_id FOREIGN KEY ("statement_id") REFERENCES statements ("statement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audio_clips_repetition_id FOREIGN KEY ("repetition_id") REFERENCES repetitions ("repetition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audio_clips_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audio_clips_actor_id FOREIGN KEY ("actor_id") REFERENCES actors ("actor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audio_clips_emotion_id FOREIGN KEY ("emotion_id") REFERENCES emotions ("emotion_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audio_clips_gender_id FOREIGN KEY ("gender_id") REFERENCES genders ("gender_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audio_clips_intensity_id FOREIGN KEY ("intensity_id") REFERENCES intensities ("intensity_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the source */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the source */,
  "last_update" TEXT /* Date when the source information was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "actors" (
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each actor */,
  "actor_name" TEXT /* Name of the actor */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Biographical information about the actor */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the actor */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the actor */,
  "experience_years" INTEGER /* Number of years of experience in voice acting */,
  "profile_image" TEXT /* Path to the actor's profile image */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("actor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genders" (
  "gender_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each gender */,
  "gender_name" TEXT /* Name of the gender */,
  "description" TEXT /* A more detailed description of the gender */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("gender_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "intensities" (
  "intensity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each intensity level */,
  "intensity_level" INTEGER /* Level of intensity (e.g., 0, 1, 2) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the intensity level */,
  "example_clip" TEXT /* Path to an example clip that best represents the intensity level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("intensity_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "statements" (
  "statement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each statement */,
  "statement_text" TEXT /* Text of the statement */,
  "context" TEXT /* Context or setting in which the statement is typically used */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the statement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("statement_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "repetitions" (
  "repetition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each repetition */,
  "repetition_number" INTEGER /* Number of the repetition */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any notes or comments about the repetition */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("repetition_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "emotions" (
  "emotion_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each emotion */,
  "emotion_name" TEXT /* Name of the emotion */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the emotion */,
  "example_clip" TEXT /* Path to an example clip that best represents the emotion */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("emotion_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "labels" (
  "label_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each label */,
  "label_name" TEXT /* Name of the label */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the label */,
  "context" TEXT /* Context or setting in which the label is typically used */,
  "example_clip" TEXT /* Path to an example clip that best represents the label */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("label_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., producer, director, data analyst, admin) */,
  "date_joined" TEXT /* Date when the user joined the system */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* User account status (e.g., active, inactive, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "clip_id" INTEGER /* ID of the audio clip being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the audio clip */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the audio clip was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  "duration" REAL /* Duration of the access session in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_clip_id FOREIGN KEY ("clip_id") REFERENCES audio_clips ("clip_id")
)
